description_long: This is a vintage wooden commode chair with a high, ornate backrest featuring a carved floral motif at the top. The seat has a circular opening, typical of historical toilet chairs, and a hinged lid that can be closed when not in use. The armrests are broad and slightly curved, providing comfort and support, while the base is boxy and sturdy, likely housing a removable chamber pot. Metal hardware, including a latch and a small handle, is visible on the side, suggesting functional parts for opening or securing the lid. The wood has a dark, aged finish with visible grain and some wear, adding to its antique character. This object would be found in historical or themed urban settings, such as museums, parks, or period-style public buildings.
description: Antique wooden commode chair with ornate backrest, armrests, and a circular seat opening, featuring carved details and metal hardware.
description_view_0: Front view showing the ornate backrest, circular seat opening, armrests, and visible metal latch.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the chair's depth, armrest profile, and metal hardware on the side.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, tall wooden back with no visible openings or hardware.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the armrest profile and the side of the seat, with less visible hardware.
